message:
fix(ci): add MinGW libKernel32.a case-sensitivity symlink for Windows cross-compilation

The MinGW sysroot ships libkernel32.a (lowercase) but some Rust crates
link against -lKernel32 (mixed-case). On case-sensitive filesystems this
causes linker failures during Windows cross-compilation. Add a symlink
step in the release workflow to create the expected mixed-case name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -134,6 +134,17 @@ jobs:
             sudo apt-get install -y mingw-w64 zip
           fi
 
+      - name: Fix MinGW import library case
+        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-pc-windows-gnu'
+        run: |
+          libkernel32="$(x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc -print-file-name=libkernel32.a)"
+          libdir="$(dirname "$libkernel32")"
+          if [ "$libkernel32" = "libkernel32.a" ] || [ ! -f "$libkernel32" ]; then
+            echo "Could not locate libkernel32.a"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          sudo ln -sf "$libkernel32" "$libdir/libKernel32.a"
+
       - name: Configure cross linker
         if: matrix.linker != ''
         run: |
